title: cyperus cyperoides var. subcompositus
intro: Cyperus cyperoides var. subcompositus, also known as the umbrella sedge or umbrella papyrus, is a type of sedge plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. It is native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world and has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including the ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support digestive health. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties and health benefits.
